message:
test(fcp-e2e): carry CapabilityConstraints in compliance tokens for every connector

Every `*_compliance_e2e.rs` test mints a CapabilityToken via
`CapabilityTokenBuilder`. Until this change, none of those tokens
supplied a constraints payload, so they depended on the host accepting
an empty constraints block. That is incompatible with the current
capability-enforcement path (fcp-core/fcp-conformance) which requires
a CBOR-encoded `CapabilityConstraints` in `.constraints_cbor()` to
evaluate any `resource_allow` check. The connector-suite runs
therefore had to lean on a compatibility shim that is being removed.

Changes applied uniformly to all 77 updated compliance tests:

- Import `CapabilityConstraints` from `fcp_core` alongside the existing
  builder imports.
- Inside each test's token factory, construct
    CapabilityConstraints {
        resource_allow: vec!["*".to_string()],
        ..Default::default()
    }
  and serialize it with `ciborium::into_writer` into a local
  `constraints_cbor: Vec<u8>`.
- Pass the bytes through
  `CapabilityTokenBuilder::constraints_cbor(&constraints_cbor)` before
  `.sign(signing_key)`. This keeps the test's intent ("any resource is
  fine for this compliance run") explicit in the token rather than
  assuming host defaults.
- For a few tests (e.g. airtable, jira, salesforce) whose connector
  adapter needs to call `handle_invoke` synchronously from a trait
  impl, wrap the `block_on_sync` call in
  `std::thread::scope(|scope| scope.spawn(...).join())` so the per-test
  runtime does not panic on reentrant `block_on` inside the
  compliance-suite worker. The wrapper translates thread-join panics
  and runtime errors into `FcpError::Internal { message }` so the test
  failure mode stays in-band.

Also updates crates/fcp-e2e/src/lib.rs:

- The inline `openai_e2e_tests` module renames its `openai.simple_chat`
  capability to `openai.chat` (and maps `openai.get_usage` onto the same
  single canonical capability) so the token it mints matches the
  manifest optional-capability names committed earlier. The test
  `build_token` helper gains the same CapabilityConstraints payload
  treatment.
- Improves the "allow suite should pass" assertion failure message to
  include the full `report` via `{report:#?}` for quicker triage when
  the suite regresses.

The `slack_e2e_tests` module picks up the same constraints pattern.

No connector code paths are touched by this commit; production
CapabilityToken-issuing code paths already supply constraints. This
aligns the test harness with how real hosts build tokens.
